summary: Postinstall script exfiltrates environment, filesystem contents, and CTF flags to hardcoded remote IP
details: On `npm install`, postinstall.js executes unconditionally on Linux hosts and harvests installer-side data: it reads process.env, runs `ls -laR /app`, captures `env` and `ps aux`, and greps `/app`, `/root`, and `/flag*` for `HTB{...}` flag patterns. The collected data is PUT as a JSON manifest to a hardcoded bare-IP endpoint at http://154.57.164.71:31289 (with localhost fallbacks). The script also contains explicit scanner-evasion logic that suppresses the payload when the working directory contains `/tmp/pkg` or the hostname matches `ec2.internal`/`amazonaws`, and only fires on Linux — designed to hide the behavior from automated analysis environments while firing on real developer/CI machines. Bare-IP destination, anti-analysis gating, environment and filesystem scraping, and CTF-flag hunting together are unambiguous supply-chain exfiltration.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
